excerpt: Enough of that "Mary Had A Little Lamb" nonsense, here's a kids song worth singing.
headline: If I Had An Army, We Would March To This Song
lede:
Malala Yousafzai almost lost her life working to give a voice to girls who are banned by the Taliban from being educated in her home country of Pakistan. She was nominated for a Nobel Prize and she has inspired countless people worldwide to stand up for what they deserve.

The refrain of this song features young girls singing "I am Malala" which I love because it reminds me of a great MLK Jr quote: "Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere."

headline: Here's Why Spending A Gajillion Dollars Keeping People Out Of The Country Is Laughable
lede: The so-called “border surge” has turned into a huge money pit, and for what? My great grandparents came here from Czechoslovakia, and odds are, yours came from somewhere else, too. What if we spent all of that money on … I don’t know, maybe job creation? Radical idea, I know.

headline: An Atheist Explains 15 Questions To Avoid Asking Him

excerpt: If you wouldn't say it to a person's face, don't say it online.
headline: Since When Does Being Popular Mean Having To Put Up With This Kind Of Bullying
lede: I love using social media to stay in touch with old friends and make connections with my new ones. For some people though, they hide behind the screen and use their anonymity to bully others. Some of our online contacts are real friends and some we hardly know. Either way, keep this in mind: if you wouldn't say something in person, it's probably not okay to say online.
share_text: This needs to stop.

headline: Here's What It Feels Like To Be Gay, If You Are Straight
lede: Being gay is hard, from what I've heard from my friends. I wouldn't know as I take my straightness for granted. It's easy to do then when I'm in the 90% majority and most people are like me in that regard. But what would it be like if 90% of the people were gay and 10% were straight? Watch and learn.

It's easy to do then when I'm in the 90% majority and most people are like me in that regard. But what would it be like if 90% of the people were gay and 10% were straight? Watch and learn.

headline: Only 1 Country Does This Terrifying Thing To 13-Year-Olds. It's The U.S.
lede:
Wow. Human rights lawyer Bryan Stevenson gives a stunning talk about injustice in our criminal system. It's a heavy topic, and there's no way to dress it up as uplifting, but at least Stevenson's charisma and engaging stories make it incredibly mesmerizing.

He starts off slow with stories of his grandmother, but by 6 minutes in he's dropping facts about our prisons that many people would much rather ignore. And it just gets better and better — don't miss the hilarious story at 17:35 about the time he asked a judge to try his client as a rich white dude. And my favorite quote of the whole talk comes at 20:35: "The moral arc of the universe is long, but it bends towards justice." Chills, people, CHILLS!

excerpt: If you wouldn't say it to a person's face, don't say it online.
headline: Since When Does Being Popular Mean Having To Put Up With This Kinda Bullying
lede: I love using social media to stay in touch with old friends and make connections with my new ones. For some people though, they hide behind the screen and use their anonymity to bully others. Some of our online contacts are real friends and some we hardly know. Either way, keep this in mind: if you wouldn't say something in person, it's probably not okay to say online.
share_text: This needs to stop.

headline: One Of The Funniest Pranks I've Seen On The Internet Teaches A Lesson On What We Don't Want To See
lede:
We love free sausage samples, but we hate seeing a piglet made into sausage, yet we keep eating the sausage. That's sorta the definition of cognitive dissonance and what happens to each of us when we mix media and politics. How many of the worlds problems would we solve if we were forced to confront the evidence in front of us?

Cognitive Dissonance: The state of having inconsistent thoughts, beliefs, or attitudes, especially as relating to behavioral decisions and attitude change.
share_text: A prank that teaches a lesson on cognitive dissonance.
